[Source](../src/rquest/lib.rs.html#1-353)

Expand description

[┬¦](#rquest)
rquest
-------------------

An ergonomic all-in-one HTTP client for browser emulation with TLS, JA3/JA4, and HTTP/2 fingerprints.

*   Plain bodies, [JSON](#json)
    , [urlencoded](#forms)
    , [multipart](multipart/index.html "mod rquest::multipart")
    
*   Cookies Store
*   Header Order
*   [Redirect Policy](#redirect-policies)
    
*   Rotating [Proxies](#proxies)
    
*   [Certificate Store](#certificate-store)
    
*   [WebSocket](#websocket)
     Upgrade
*   HTTPS via [BoringSSL](#tls)
    
*   HTTP/2 over TLS [Emulation](#emulation)
    

Additional learning resources include:

*   [The Rust Cookbook](https://doc.rust-lang.org/stable/book/ch00-00-introduction.html)
    
*   [Repository Examples](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest/tree/main/examples)
    

### [┬¦](#emulation)
Emulation

The `emulation` module provides a way to simulate various browser TLS/HTTP2 fingerprints.

    use rquest::Client;
    use rquest_util::Emulation;
    
    #[tokio::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rquest::Error> {
        // Build a client
        let client = Client::builder()
            .emulation(Emulation::Firefox136)
            .build()?;
    
        // Use the API you're already familiar with
        let resp = client.get("https://tls.peet.ws/api/all").send().await?;
        println!("{}", resp.text().await?);
    
        Ok(())
    }

### [┬¦](#websocket)
Websocket

The `websocket` module provides a way to upgrade a connection to a websocket.

    use futures_util::{SinkExt, StreamExt, TryStreamExt};
    use http::header;
    use rquest::{Client, Message};
    use std::time::Duration;
    
    #[tokio::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rquest::Error> {
       // Build a client
       let client = Client::builder()
           .cert_verification(false)
           .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10))
           .build()?;
    
       // Use the API you're already familiar with
       let websocket = client
           .websocket("wss://echo.websocket.org")
           .header(header::USER_AGENT, env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"))
           .send()
           .await?;
    
       assert_eq!(websocket.version(), http::Version::HTTP_11);
    
       let (mut tx, mut rx) = websocket.into_websocket().await?.split();
    
       tokio::spawn(async move {
           for i in 1..11 {
               if let Err(err) = tx.send(Message::text(format!("Hello, World! {i}"))).await {
                   eprintln!("failed to send message: {err}");
               }
           }
       });
    
       while let Some(message) = rx.try_next().await? {
           if let Message::Text(text) = message {
               println!("received: {text}");
           }
       }
    
       Ok(())
    }

### [┬¦](#making-a-get-request)
Making a GET request

Making a GET request is simple.

    let body = rquest::Client::new()
        .get("https://www.rust-lang.org")
        .send()
        .await?
        .text()
        .await?;
    
    println!("body = {:?}", body);

**NOTE**: If you plan to perform multiple requests, it is best to create a [`Client`](./struct.Client.html)
 and reuse it, taking advantage of keep-alive connection pooling.

### [┬¦](#making-post-requests-or-setting-request-bodies)
Making POST requests (or setting request bodies)

There are several ways you can set the body of a request. The basic one is by using the `body()` method of a [`RequestBuilder`](./struct.RequestBuilder.html)
. This lets you set the exact raw bytes of what the body should be. It accepts various types, including `String` and `Vec<u8>`. If you wish to pass a custom type, you can use the `rquest::Body` constructors.

    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let res = client.post("http://httpbin.org/post")
        .body("the exact body that is sent")
        .send()
        .await?;

#### [┬¦](#forms)
Forms

ItŌĆÖs very common to want to send form data in a request body. This can be done with any type that can be serialized into form data.

This can be an array of tuples, or a `HashMap`, or a custom type that implements [`Serialize`](http://serde.rs)
.

    // This will POST a body of `foo=bar&baz=quux`
    let params = [("foo", "bar"), ("baz", "quux")];
    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let res = client.post("http://httpbin.org/post")
        .form(&params)
        .send()
        .await?;

#### [┬¦](#json)
JSON

There is also a `json` method helper on the [`RequestBuilder`](./struct.RequestBuilder.html)
 that works in a similar fashion the `form` method. It can take any value that can be serialized into JSON. The feature `json` is required.

    // This will POST a body of `{"lang":"rust","body":"json"}`
    let mut map = HashMap::new();
    map.insert("lang", "rust");
    map.insert("body", "json");
    
    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let res = client.post("http://httpbin.org/post")
        .json(&map)
        .send()
        .await?;

### [┬¦](#redirect-policies)
Redirect Policies

By default, the client does not handle HTTP redirects. To customize this behavior, you can use [`redirect::Policy`](redirect/index.html "mod rquest::redirect")
 with ClientBuilder.

### [┬¦](#cookies)
Cookies

The automatic storing and sending of session cookies can be enabled with the [`cookie_store`](struct.ClientBuilder.html#method.cookie_store "method rquest::ClientBuilder::cookie_store")
 method on `ClientBuilder`.

### [┬¦](#proxies)
Proxies

**NOTE**: System proxies are enabled by default.

System proxies look in environment variables to set HTTP or HTTPS proxies.

`HTTP_PROXY` or `http_proxy` provide HTTP proxies for HTTP connections while `HTTPS_PROXY` or `https_proxy` provide HTTPS proxies for HTTPS connections. `ALL_PROXY` or `all_proxy` provide proxies for both HTTP and HTTPS connections. If both the all proxy and HTTP or HTTPS proxy variables are set the more specific HTTP or HTTPS proxies take precedence.

These can be overwritten by adding a [`Proxy`](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")
 to `ClientBuilder` i.e. `let proxy = rquest::Proxy::http("https://secure.example")?;` or disabled by calling `ClientBuilder::no_proxy()`.

`socks` feature is required if you have configured socks proxy like this:

    export https_proxy=socks5://127.0.0.1:1086

*   `http://` is the scheme for http proxy
*   `https://` is the scheme for https proxy
*   `socks4://` is the scheme for socks4 proxy
*   `socks4a://` is the scheme for socks4a proxy
*   `socks5://` is the scheme for socks5 proxy
*   `socks5h://` is the scheme for socks5h proxy

### [┬¦](#tls)
TLS

By default, clients will utilize BoringSSL transport layer security to connect to HTTPS targets.

*   Various parts of TLS can also be configured or even disabled on the `ClientBuilder`.

### [┬¦](#certificate-store)
Certificate Store

By default, rquest uses MozillaŌĆÖs root certificates through the webpki-roots crate. This static root certificate bundle is not automatically updated and ignores any root certificates installed on the host. You can disable default-features to use the systemŌĆÖs default certificate path. Additionally, rquest provides a certificate store for users to customize and update certificates.

Custom Certificate Store verification supports Root CA certificates, peer certificates, and self-signed certificate SSL pinning.

### [┬¦](#optional-features)
Optional Features

The following are a list of [Cargo features](https://doc.rust-lang.org/stable/cargo/reference/manifest.html#the-features-section)
 that can be enabled or disabled:

*   **full**: Enables all optional features.
*   **websocket**: Provides websocket support.
*   **cookies**: Provides cookie session support.
*   **cookies-preserve-order**: uses [indexmap::IndexMap](https://docs.rs/indexmap/latest/indexmap/map/struct.IndexMap.html)
     in lieu of [HashMap](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/collections/struct.HashMap.html)
     internally, so cookies are maintained in insertion/creation order.
*   **cookies-abstract**: Provides abstract cookie session support.
*   **cookies-multiple**: By default, all cookies are squeezed and sent. Enabling this feature will send multiple cookie headers.
*   **gzip**: Provides response body gzip decompression.
*   **brotli**: Provides response body brotli decompression.
*   **zstd**: Provides response body zstd decompression.
*   **deflate**: Provides response body deflate decompression.
*   **json**: Provides serialization and deserialization for JSON bodies.
*   **multipart**: Provides functionality for multipart forms.
*   **stream**: Adds support for `futures::Stream`.
*   **socks**: Provides SOCKS5 proxy support.
*   **hickory-dns**: Enables a hickory-dns async resolver instead of default threadpool using `getaddrinfo`.
*   **native-roots**: Use the native system root certificate store.
*   **webpki-roots**: Use the webpki-roots crate for root certificates.
*   **apple-network-device-binding**: Use the Apple Network Device Binding
*   **http2-tracing**: Enable HTTP/2 tracing.
*   **internal\_proxy\_sys\_no\_cache**: Use the internal proxy system with no cache.

[Priority](struct.Priority.html "struct rquest::Priority")

The PRIORITY frame (type=0x2) specifies the sender-advised priority of a stream \[Section 5.3\]. It can be sent in any stream state, including idle or closed streams. \[Section 5.3\]: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7540#section-5.3

A parsed URL record.

[Utf8Bytes](struct.Utf8Bytes.html "struct rquest::Utf8Bytes")

UTF-8 wrapper for [Bytes](https://docs.rs/bytes/1.10.1/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bytes/bytes/struct.Bytes.html "struct bytes::bytes::Bytes")
.

[Version](struct.Version.html "struct rquest::Version")

Represents a version of the HTTP spec.

[WebSocket](struct.WebSocket.html "struct rquest::WebSocket")

A websocket connection

[WebSocketRequestBuilder](struct.WebSocketRequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::WebSocketRequestBuilder")

Wrapper for [`RequestBuilder`](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")
 that performs the websocket handshake when sent.

[WebSocketResponse](struct.WebSocketResponse.html "struct rquest::WebSocketResponse")

The serverŌĆÖs response to the websocket upgrade request.

Enums[┬¦](#enums)

-----------------

[CertCompressionAlgorithm](enum.CertCompressionAlgorithm.html "enum rquest::CertCompressionAlgorithm")

IANA assigned identifier of compression algorithm. See https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8879.html#name-compression-algorithms

[Message](enum.Message.html "enum rquest::Message")

A WebSocket message.

[PseudoOrder](enum.PseudoOrder.html "enum rquest::PseudoOrder")

Represents the order of HTTP/2 pseudo-header fields in the header block.

[SettingsOrder](enum.SettingsOrder.html "enum rquest::SettingsOrder")

Represents the order of settings parameters in an HTTP/2 SETTINGS frame.

Traits[┬¦](#traits)

-------------------

[EmulationProviderFactory](trait.EmulationProviderFactory.html "trait rquest::EmulationProviderFactory")

Trait defining the interface for providing an `EmulationProvider`.

[IntoUrl](trait.IntoUrl.html "trait rquest::IntoUrl")

A trait to try to convert some type into a `Url`.

[ResponseBuilderExt](trait.ResponseBuilderExt.html "trait rquest::ResponseBuilderExt")

Extension trait for http::response::Builder objects

Type Aliases[┬¦](#types)

------------------------

[Result](type.Result.html "type rquest::Result")

A `Result` alias where the `Err` case is `rquest::Error`.

*    [![0x676e67](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/51810656?v=4)](https://crates.io/users/0x676e67)

rquest
======

[![CI](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
 [![Crates.io License](https://img.shields.io/crates/l/rquest)](./LICENSE)
 ![Crates.io MSRV](https://img.shields.io/crates/msrv/rquest?logo=rust) [![crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/rquest.svg?logo=rust)](https://crates.io/crates/rquest)
 [![Crates.io Total Downloads](https://img.shields.io/crates/d/rquest)](https://crates.io/crates/rquest)

> ­¤ÜĆ Help me work seamlessly with open source sharing by [sponsoring me on GitHub](https://github.com/0x676e67/0x676e67/blob/main/SPONSOR.md)

An ergonomic all-in-one HTTP client for browser emulation with TLS, JA3/JA4, and HTTP/2 fingerprints.

Features
--------

*   Plain bodies, JSON, urlencoded, multipart
*   Cookie Store
*   Header Order
*   Redirect Policy
*   Rotating Proxies
*   Certificate Store
*   WebSocket Upgrade
*   HTTPS via BoringSSL
*   HTTP/2 over TLS Emulation

Example
-------

This asynchronous example utilizes [Tokio](https://tokio.rs)
 with optional features enabled, requiring the following configuration in `Cargo.toml`:

    [dependencies]
    tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
    rquest = "5"
    rquest-util = "2"
    

And then the code:

    use rquest::Client;
    use rquest_util::Emulation;
    
    #[tokio::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rquest::Error> {
        // Build a client
        let client = Client::builder()
            .emulation(Emulation::Firefox136)
            .build()?;
    
        // Use the API you're already familiar with
        let resp = client.get("https://tls.peet.ws/api/all").send().await?;
        println!("{}", resp.text().await?);
    
        Ok(())
    }
    

Emulation
---------

*   **HTTP/2 over TLS**
    
    **JA3**/**JA4**/**Akamai** fingerprints cannot accurately simulate browser fingerprints due to the sophistication of TLS encryption and the popularity of HTTP/2. rquest does not plan to support parsing these fingerprint strings for simulation. Users are encouraged to customize the configuration according to their own needs.
    
*   **Emulation Device**
    
    Most browser device models share the same TLS and HTTP/2 configuration, differing only in the User-Agent. The browser device emulation template is managed by [rquest-util](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest-util)
    .
    

Building
--------

Avoid compiling with packages that depend on `openssl-sys`, as it shares the same prefix symbol with `boring-sys`, potentially leading to [link failures](https://github.com/cloudflare/boring/issues/197)
 and other issues. Even if compilation succeeds, using both `openssl-sys` and `boring-sys` together can result in memory segmentation faults. Until the upstream Boring resolves these linking conflicts, using `rustls` is the best workaround.

Install the dependencies required to build [BoringSSL](https://github.com/google/boringssl/blob/master/BUILDING.md#build-prerequisites)

    sudo apt-get install build-essential cmake perl pkg-config libclang-dev musl-tools -y
    
    cargo build --release
    

This GitHub Actions [workflow](.github/compilation-guide/build.yml)
 can be used to compile the project on **Linux**, **Windows**, and **macOS**.

License
-------

Released under the [Apache-2.0](./LICENSE)
 License.

Contribution
------------

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work by you, as defined in the [Apache-2.0](./LICENSE)
 license, shall be licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

Sponsors
--------

[CapSolver](https://www.capsolver.com/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=rquest)
 leverages AI-powered Auto Web Unblock to bypass Captchas effortlessly, providing fast, reliable, and cost-effective data access with seamless integration into Colly, Puppeteer, and PlaywrightŌĆöuse code **`RQUEST`** for a 6% bonus!

Accolades
---------

The project is based on a fork of [reqwest](https://github.com/seanmonstar/reqwest)
.

[rquest](../index.html)

Module redirectCopy item path
=============================

[Source](../../src/rquest/redirect.rs.html#1-394)

Expand description

Redirect Handling

By default, a `Client` will automatically handle HTTP redirects, having a maximum redirect chain of 10 hops. To customize this behavior, a `redirect::Policy` can be used with a `ClientBuilder`.

Structs[┬¦](#structs)

---------------------

[Action](struct.Action.html "struct rquest::redirect::Action")

An action to perform when a redirect status code is found.

[Attempt](struct.Attempt.html "struct rquest::redirect::Attempt")

A type that holds information on the next request and previous requests in redirect chain.

[Policy](struct.Policy.html "struct rquest::redirect::Policy")

A type that controls the policy on how to handle the following of redirects.

[rquest](index.html)

Struct ClientCopy item path
===========================

[Source](../src/rquest/client/http.rs.html#94-96)

    pub struct Client { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

An asynchronous `Client` to make Requests with.

The Client has various configuration values to tweak, but the defaults are set to what is usually the most commonly desired value. To configure a `Client`, use `Client::builder()`.

The `Client` holds a connection pool internally, so it is advised that you create one and **reuse** it.

You do **not** have to wrap the `Client` in an [`Rc`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/rc/struct.Rc.html "struct alloc::rc::Rc")
 or [`Arc`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/sync/struct.Arc.html "struct alloc::sync::Arc")
 to **reuse** it, because it already uses an [`Arc`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/sync/struct.Arc.html "struct alloc::sync::Arc")
 internally.

#### pub fn [new](#method.new)
() -> [Client](struct.Client.html "struct rquest::Client")

Constructs a new `Client`.

##### [┬¦](#panics)
Panics

This method panics if a TLS backend cannot be initialized, or the resolver cannot load the system configuration.

Use `Client::builder()` if you wish to handle the failure as an `Error` instead of panicking.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/http.rs.html#1311-1313)

#### pub fn [builder](#method.builder)
() -> [ClientBuilder](struct.ClientBuilder.html "struct rquest::ClientBuilder")

Create a `ClientBuilder` specifically configured for WebSocket connections.

This method configures the `ClientBuilder` to use HTTP/1.0 only, which is required for certain WebSocket connections.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/http.rs.html#1320-1322)

Upgrades the [`RequestBuilder`](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

#### pub fn [execute](#method.execute)
( &self, request: [Request](struct.Request.html "struct rquest::Request")
, ) -> impl [Future](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/future/future/trait.Future.html "trait core::future::future::Future")
<Output = [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Response](struct.Response.html "struct rquest::Response")
, [Error](struct.Error.html "struct rquest::Error")
\>>

Executes a `Request`.

A `Request` can be built manually with `Request::new()` or obtained from a RequestBuilder with `RequestBuilder::build()`.

You should prefer to use the `RequestBuilder` and `RequestBuilder::send()`.

##### [┬¦](#errors-7)
Errors

This method fails if there was an error while sending request, redirect loop was detected or redirect limit was exhausted.

#### pub fn [set\_cookies](#method.set_cookies)
<C>(&self, url: &[Url](struct.Url.html "struct rquest::Url")
, cookies: C)

where C: [AsRef](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.AsRef.html "trait core::convert::AsRef")
<\[[HeaderValue](header/struct.HeaderValue.html "struct rquest::header::HeaderValue")\
\]>,

Injects a ŌĆśCookieŌĆÖ into the ŌĆśCookieStoreŌĆÖ for the specified URL.

This method accepts a collection of cookies, which can be either an owned vector (`Vec<HeaderValue>`) or a reference to a slice (`&[HeaderValue]`). It will convert the collection into an iterator and pass it to the `cookie_store` for processing.

##### [┬¦](#parameters)
Parameters

*   `url`: The URL associated with the cookies to be set.
*   `cookies`: A collection of `HeaderValue` items, either by reference or owned.

This method ensures that cookies are only set if at least one cookie exists in the collection.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/http.rs.html#1599-1606)

#### pub fn [set\_cookie](#method.set_cookie)
<'c, C>(&self, url: &[Url](struct.Url.html "struct rquest::Url")
, cookie: C)

where C: [IntoCookie](cookie/trait.IntoCookie.html "trait rquest::cookie::IntoCookie")
 + 'c,

Inserts a cookie into the `CookieStore` for the specified URL.

##### [┬¦](#note)
Note

This method requires the `cookies` feature to be enabled.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/http.rs.html#1624-1628)

#### pub fn [remove\_cookie](#method.remove_cookie)
(&self, url: &[Url](struct.Url.html "struct rquest::Url")
, name: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
)

Removes a cookie from the `CookieStore` for the specified URL.

This method deletes a cookie with the given name from the clientŌĆÖs `CookieStore` for the specified URL. It can be useful in scenarios where you want to remove specific cookies to reset the clientŌĆÖs state or ensure that certain cookies are not sent with subsequent requests.

##### [┬¦](#parameters-1)
Parameters

*   `url`: The URL associated with the cookie to be removed.
*   `name`: The name of the cookie to be removed.

##### [┬¦](#note-1)
Note

This method requires the `cookies` feature to be enabled.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/http.rs.html#1640-1644)

#### pub fn [clear\_cookies](#method.clear_cookies)
(&self)

Clears all cookies from the `CookieStore`.

This method removes all cookies stored in the clientŌĆÖs `CookieStore`. It can be useful in scenarios where you want to reset the clientŌĆÖs state or ensure that no cookies are sent with subsequent requests.

##### [┬¦](#note-2)
Note

This method requires the `cookies` feature to be enabled.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/http.rs.html#1668-1674)

#### pub fn [update](#method.update)
(&self) -> [ClientUpdate](struct.ClientUpdate.html "struct rquest::ClientUpdate")
<'\_>

Returns a `ClientUpdate` instance to modify the internal state of the `Client`.

This method allows you to obtain a `ClientUpdate` instance, which provides methods to mutate the internal state of the `Client`. This is useful when you need to modify the clientŌĆÖs configuration or state after it has been created.

##### [┬¦](#returns-2)
Returns

A `ClientUpdate<'_>` instance that can be used to modify the internal state of the `Client`.

##### [┬¦](#example)
Example

    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    client.update()
        .headers(|headers| {
            headers.insert("X-Custom-Header", HeaderValue::from_static("value"));
        })
        .apply()
        .unwrap();

[Source](../src/rquest/client/http.rs.html#1691-1695)

#### pub fn [cloned](#method.cloned)
(&self) -> Self

Clones the `Client` into a new instance.

This method creates a new instance of the `Client` by cloning its internal state. The cloned client will have the same configuration and state as the original client, but it will be a separate instance that can be used independently. Note that this will still share the connection pool with the original `Client`.

##### [┬¦](#example-1)
Example

    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let cloned_client = client.cloned();
    // Use the cloned client independently

<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

<Self, Self>

(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

    pub struct RequestBuilder { /* private fields */ }

A builder to construct the properties of a `Request`.

To construct a `RequestBuilder`, refer to the `Client` documentation.

#### pub fn [basic\_auth](#method.basic_auth)
<U, P>( self, username: U, password: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<P>, ) -> [RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

where U: [Display](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/fmt/trait.Display.html "trait core::fmt::Display")
, P: [Display](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/fmt/trait.Display.html "trait core::fmt::Display")
,

Enable HTTP basic authentication.

    
    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let resp = client.delete("http://httpbin.org/delete")
        .basic_auth("admin", Some("good password"))
        .send()
        .await?;

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#421-426)

#### pub fn [timeout](#method.timeout)
(self, timeout: [Duration](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/time/struct.Duration.html "struct core::time::Duration")
) -> [RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

Enables a request timeout.

The timeout is applied from when the request starts connecting until the response body has finished. It affects only this request and overrides the timeout configured using `ClientBuilder::timeout()`.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#433-438)

#### pub fn [read\_timeout](#method.read_timeout)
(self, timeout: [Duration](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/time/struct.Duration.html "struct core::time::Duration")
) -> [RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

Enables a read timeout.

The read timeout is applied from when the response body starts being read until the response body has finished. It affects only this request and overrides the read timeout configured using `ClientBuilder::read_timeout()`.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#461-479)

#### pub fn [multipart](#method.multipart)
(self, multipart: [Form](multipart/struct.Form.html "struct rquest::multipart::Form")
) -> [RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

Available on **crate feature `multipart`** only.

Sends a multipart/form-data body.

    
    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let form = rquest::multipart::Form::new()
        .text("key3", "value3")
        .text("key4", "value4");
    
    
    let response = client.post("your url")
        .multipart(form)
        .send()
        .await?;

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#499-519)

#### pub fn [query](#method.query)
<T: [Serialize](https://docs.rs/serde/1.0.219/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/serde/ser/trait.Serialize.html "trait serde::ser::Serialize")
 + ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
\>(self, query: [&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
) -> [RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

Modify the query string of the URL.

Modifies the URL of this request, adding the parameters provided. This method appends and does not overwrite. This means that it can be called multiple times and that existing query parameters are not overwritten if the same key is used. The key will simply show up twice in the query string. Calling `.query(&[("foo", "a"), ("foo", "b")])` gives `"foo=a&foo=b"`.

##### [┬¦](#note)
Note

This method does not support serializing a single key-value pair. Instead of using `.query(("key", "val"))`, use a sequence, such as `.query(&[("key", "val")])`. ItŌĆÖs also possible to serialize structs and maps into a key-value pair.

##### [┬¦](#errors)
Errors

This method will fail if the object you provide cannot be serialized into a query string.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#522-527)

#### pub fn [version](#method.version)
(self, version: [Version](struct.Version.html "struct rquest::Version")
) -> [RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

Set HTTP version

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#530-535)

#### pub fn [redirect](#method.redirect)
(self, policy: [Policy](redirect/struct.Policy.html "struct rquest::redirect::Policy")
) -> [RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

Set the redirect policy for this request.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#547-552)

#### pub fn [allow\_compression](#method.allow_compression)
(self, allow\_compression: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> Self

Sets if this request will announce that it accepts compression.

This value defaults to true. Note that this only lets the browser know that this request supports compression, the server might choose not to compress the content.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#577-594)

#### pub fn [proxy](#method.proxy)
<P>(self, proxy: P) -> [RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

where P: IntoProxy,

Set the proxy for this request.

##### [┬¦](#examples)
Examples

    use rquest::Client;
    use rquest::Proxy;
    
    let client = Client::new();
    let proxy = Proxy::all("http://hyper.rs/prox")?.basic_auth("Aladdin", "open sesame");
    
    let resp = client
        .get("https://tls.peet.ws/api/all")
        .proxy(proxy)
        .send()
        .await?;
    
    let resp = client
        .get("https://tls.peet.ws/api/all")
        .proxy("http://hyper.rs/prox")
        .send()
        .await?;

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#597-605)

#### pub fn [form](#method.form)
<T: [Serialize](https://docs.rs/serde/1.0.219/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/serde/ser/trait.Serialize.html "trait serde::ser::Serialize")
 + ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
\>(self, form: [&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
) -> [RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

Send a form body.

Sets the body to the url encoded serialization of the passed value, and also sets the `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` header.

    let mut params = HashMap::new();
    params.insert("lang", "rust");
    
    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let res = client.post("http://httpbin.org")
        .form(&params)
        .send()
        .await?;

##### [┬¦](#errors-1)
Errors

This method fails if the passed value cannot be serialized into url encoded format

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#702-716)

#### pub fn [json](#method.json)
<T: [Serialize](https://docs.rs/serde/1.0.219/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/serde/ser/trait.Serialize.html "trait serde::ser::Serialize")
 + ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
\>(self, json: [&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
) -> [RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

Available on **crate feature `json`** only.

Send a JSON body.

##### [┬¦](#optional)
Optional

This requires the optional `json` feature enabled.

##### [┬¦](#errors-2)
Errors

Serialization can fail if `T`ŌĆÖs implementation of `Serialize` decides to fail, or if `T` contains a map with non-string keys.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#720-722)

#### pub fn [build](#method.build)
(self) -> [Result](type.Result.html "type rquest::Result")
<[Request](struct.Request.html "struct rquest::Request")
\>

Build a `Request`, which can be inspected, modified and executed with `Client::execute()`.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#729-731)

#### pub fn [build\_split](#method.build_split)
(self) -> ([Client](struct.Client.html "struct rquest::Client")
, [Result](type.Result.html "type rquest::Result")
<[Request](struct.Request.html "struct rquest::Request")
\>)

Build a `Request`, which can be inspected, modified and executed with `Client::execute()`.

This is similar to [`RequestBuilder::build()`](struct.RequestBuilder.html#method.build "method rquest::RequestBuilder::build")
, but also returns the embedded `Client`.

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#754-759)

#### pub fn [send](#method.send)
(self) -> impl [Future](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/future/future/trait.Future.html "trait core::future::future::Future")
<Output = [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Response](struct.Response.html "struct rquest::Response")
, [Error](struct.Error.html "struct rquest::Error")
\>>

Constructs the Request and sends it to the target URL, returning a future Response.

##### [┬¦](#errors-3)
Errors

This method fails if there was an error while sending request, redirect loop was detected or redirect limit was exhausted.

##### [┬¦](#example)
Example

    let response = rquest::Client::new()
        .get("https://hyper.rs")
        .send()
        .await?;

[Source](../src/rquest/client/request.rs.html#780-789)

#### pub fn [try\_clone](#method.try_clone)
(&self) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")
\>

Attempt to clone the RequestBuilder.

`None` is returned if the RequestBuilder can not be cloned, i.e. if the request body is a stream.

##### [┬¦](#examples-1)
Examples

    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let builder = client.post("http://httpbin.org/post")
        .body("from a &str!");
    let clone = builder.try_clone();
    assert!(clone.is_some());

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")

 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[┬¦](#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

Crate rquestCopy item path
==========================

[Source](../src/rquest/lib.rs.html#1-353)

Expand description

[┬¦](#rquest)
rquest
-------------------

An ergonomic all-in-one HTTP client for browser emulation with TLS, JA3/JA4, and HTTP/2 fingerprints.

*   Plain bodies, [JSON](#json)
    , [urlencoded](#forms)
    , [multipart](multipart/index.html "mod rquest::multipart")
    
*   Cookies Store
*   Header Order
*   [Redirect Policy](#redirect-policies)
    
*   Rotating [Proxies](#proxies)
    
*   [Certificate Store](#certificate-store)
    
*   [WebSocket](#websocket)
     Upgrade
*   HTTPS via [BoringSSL](#tls)
    
*   HTTP/2 over TLS [Emulation](#emulation)
    

Additional learning resources include:

*   [The Rust Cookbook](https://doc.rust-lang.org/stable/book/ch00-00-introduction.html)
    
*   [Repository Examples](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest/tree/main/examples)
    

### [┬¦](#emulation)
Emulation

The `emulation` module provides a way to simulate various browser TLS/HTTP2 fingerprints.

    use rquest::Client;
    use rquest_util::Emulation;
    
    #[tokio::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rquest::Error> {
        // Build a client
        let client = Client::builder()
            .emulation(Emulation::Firefox136)
            .build()?;
    
        // Use the API you're already familiar with
        let resp = client.get("https://tls.peet.ws/api/all").send().await?;
        println!("{}", resp.text().await?);
    
        Ok(())
    }

### [┬¦](#websocket)
Websocket

The `websocket` module provides a way to upgrade a connection to a websocket.

    use futures_util::{SinkExt, StreamExt, TryStreamExt};
    use http::header;
    use rquest::{Client, Message};
    use std::time::Duration;
    
    #[tokio::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rquest::Error> {
       // Build a client
       let client = Client::builder()
           .cert_verification(false)
           .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10))
           .build()?;
    
       // Use the API you're already familiar with
       let websocket = client
           .websocket("wss://echo.websocket.org")
           .header(header::USER_AGENT, env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"))
           .send()
           .await?;
    
       assert_eq!(websocket.version(), http::Version::HTTP_11);
    
       let (mut tx, mut rx) = websocket.into_websocket().await?.split();
    
       tokio::spawn(async move {
           for i in 1..11 {
               if let Err(err) = tx.send(Message::text(format!("Hello, World! {i}"))).await {
                   eprintln!("failed to send message: {err}");
               }
           }
       });
    
       while let Some(message) = rx.try_next().await? {
           if let Message::Text(text) = message {
               println!("received: {text}");
           }
       }
    
       Ok(())
    }

### [┬¦](#making-a-get-request)
Making a GET request

Making a GET request is simple.

    let body = rquest::Client::new()
        .get("https://www.rust-lang.org")
        .send()
        .await?
        .text()
        .await?;
    
    println!("body = {:?}", body);

**NOTE**: If you plan to perform multiple requests, it is best to create a [`Client`](./struct.Client.html)
 and reuse it, taking advantage of keep-alive connection pooling.

### [┬¦](#making-post-requests-or-setting-request-bodies)
Making POST requests (or setting request bodies)

There are several ways you can set the body of a request. The basic one is by using the `body()` method of a [`RequestBuilder`](./struct.RequestBuilder.html)
. This lets you set the exact raw bytes of what the body should be. It accepts various types, including `String` and `Vec<u8>`. If you wish to pass a custom type, you can use the `rquest::Body` constructors.

    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let res = client.post("http://httpbin.org/post")
        .body("the exact body that is sent")
        .send()
        .await?;

#### [┬¦](#forms)
Forms

ItŌĆÖs very common to want to send form data in a request body. This can be done with any type that can be serialized into form data.

This can be an array of tuples, or a `HashMap`, or a custom type that implements [`Serialize`](http://serde.rs)
.

    // This will POST a body of `foo=bar&baz=quux`
    let params = [("foo", "bar"), ("baz", "quux")];
    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let res = client.post("http://httpbin.org/post")
        .form(&params)
        .send()
        .await?;

#### [┬¦](#json)
JSON

There is also a `json` method helper on the [`RequestBuilder`](./struct.RequestBuilder.html)
 that works in a similar fashion the `form` method. It can take any value that can be serialized into JSON. The feature `json` is required.

    // This will POST a body of `{"lang":"rust","body":"json"}`
    let mut map = HashMap::new();
    map.insert("lang", "rust");
    map.insert("body", "json");
    
    let client = rquest::Client::new();
    let res = client.post("http://httpbin.org/post")
        .json(&map)
        .send()
        .await?;

### [┬¦](#redirect-policies)
Redirect Policies

By default, the client does not handle HTTP redirects. To customize this behavior, you can use [`redirect::Policy`](redirect/index.html "mod rquest::redirect")
 with ClientBuilder.

### [┬¦](#cookies)
Cookies

The automatic storing and sending of session cookies can be enabled with the [`cookie_store`](struct.ClientBuilder.html#method.cookie_store "method rquest::ClientBuilder::cookie_store")
 method on `ClientBuilder`.

### [┬¦](#proxies)
Proxies

**NOTE**: System proxies are enabled by default.

System proxies look in environment variables to set HTTP or HTTPS proxies.

`HTTP_PROXY` or `http_proxy` provide HTTP proxies for HTTP connections while `HTTPS_PROXY` or `https_proxy` provide HTTPS proxies for HTTPS connections. `ALL_PROXY` or `all_proxy` provide proxies for both HTTP and HTTPS connections. If both the all proxy and HTTP or HTTPS proxy variables are set the more specific HTTP or HTTPS proxies take precedence.

These can be overwritten by adding a [`Proxy`](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")
 to `ClientBuilder` i.e. `let proxy = rquest::Proxy::http("https://secure.example")?;` or disabled by calling `ClientBuilder::no_proxy()`.

`socks` feature is required if you have configured socks proxy like this:

    export https_proxy=socks5://127.0.0.1:1086

*   `http://` is the scheme for http proxy
*   `https://` is the scheme for https proxy
*   `socks4://` is the scheme for socks4 proxy
*   `socks4a://` is the scheme for socks4a proxy
*   `socks5://` is the scheme for socks5 proxy
*   `socks5h://` is the scheme for socks5h proxy

### [┬¦](#tls)
TLS

By default, clients will utilize BoringSSL transport layer security to connect to HTTPS targets.

*   Various parts of TLS can also be configured or even disabled on the `ClientBuilder`.

### [┬¦](#certificate-store)
Certificate Store

By default, rquest uses MozillaŌĆÖs root certificates through the webpki-roots crate. This static root certificate bundle is not automatically updated and ignores any root certificates installed on the host. You can disable default-features to use the systemŌĆÖs default certificate path. Additionally, rquest provides a certificate store for users to customize and update certificates.

Custom Certificate Store verification supports Root CA certificates, peer certificates, and self-signed certificate SSL pinning.

### [┬¦](#optional-features)
Optional Features

The following are a list of [Cargo features](https://doc.rust-lang.org/stable/cargo/reference/manifest.html#the-features-section)
 that can be enabled or disabled:

*   **full**: Enables all optional features.
*   **websocket**: Provides websocket support.
*   **cookies**: Provides cookie session support.
*   **cookies-preserve-order**: uses [indexmap::IndexMap](https://docs.rs/indexmap/latest/indexmap/map/struct.IndexMap.html)
     in lieu of [HashMap](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/collections/struct.HashMap.html)
     internally, so cookies are maintained in insertion/creation order.
*   **cookies-abstract**: Provides abstract cookie session support.
*   **cookies-multiple**: By default, all cookies are squeezed and sent. Enabling this feature will send multiple cookie headers.
*   **gzip**: Provides response body gzip decompression.
*   **brotli**: Provides response body brotli decompression.
*   **zstd**: Provides response body zstd decompression.
*   **deflate**: Provides response body deflate decompression.
*   **json**: Provides serialization and deserialization for JSON bodies.
*   **multipart**: Provides functionality for multipart forms.
*   **stream**: Adds support for `futures::Stream`.
*   **socks**: Provides SOCKS5 proxy support.
*   **hickory-dns**: Enables a hickory-dns async resolver instead of default threadpool using `getaddrinfo`.
*   **native-roots**: Use the native system root certificate store.
*   **webpki-roots**: Use the webpki-roots crate for root certificates.
*   **apple-network-device-binding**: Use the Apple Network Device Binding
*   **http2-tracing**: Enable HTTP/2 tracing.
*   **internal\_proxy\_sys\_no\_cache**: Use the internal proxy system with no cache.

Structs[┬¦](#structs)

---------------------

[Body](struct.Body.html "struct rquest::Body")

An asynchronous request body.

[Client](struct.Client.html "struct rquest::Client")

An asynchronous `Client` to make Requests with.

[ClientBuilder](struct.ClientBuilder.html "struct rquest::ClientBuilder")

A `ClientBuilder` can be used to create a `Client` with custom configuration.

[ClientUpdate](struct.ClientUpdate.html "struct rquest::ClientUpdate")

A mutable reference to a `ClientRef`.

[CloseCode](struct.CloseCode.html "struct rquest::CloseCode")

Status code used to indicate why an endpoint is closing the WebSocket connection.

[CloseFrame](struct.CloseFrame.html "struct rquest::CloseFrame")

A struct representing the close command.

[Dst](struct.Dst.html "struct rquest::Dst")

Destination of the request.

[EmulationProvider](struct.EmulationProvider.html "struct rquest::EmulationProvider")

HTTP connection context that manages both HTTP and TLS configurations.

[Error](struct.Error.html "struct rquest::Error")

The Errors that may occur when processing a `Request`.

[ExtensionType](struct.ExtensionType.html "struct rquest::ExtensionType")

Extension types, to be used with `ClientHello::get_extension`.

[Http1Builder](struct.Http1Builder.html "struct rquest::Http1Builder")

Http1 part of builder.

[Http1Config](struct.Http1Config.html "struct rquest::Http1Config")

Configuration config for HTTP/1 connections.

[Http2Builder](struct.Http2Builder.html "struct rquest::Http2Builder")

Http2 part of builder.

[Http2Config](struct.Http2Config.html "struct rquest::Http2Config")

Configuration config for an HTTP/2 connection.

[Method](struct.Method.html "struct rquest::Method")

The Request Method (VERB)

[NoProxy](struct.NoProxy.html "struct rquest::NoProxy")

A configuration for filtering out requests that shouldnŌĆÖt be proxied

[Priority](struct.Priority.html "struct rquest::Priority")

The PRIORITY frame (type=0x2) specifies the sender-advised priority of a stream \[Section 5.3\]. It can be sent in any stream state, including idle or closed streams. \[Section 5.3\]: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7540#section-5.3

[Proxy](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")

Configuration of a proxy that a `Client` should pass requests to.

[Request](struct.Request.html "struct rquest::Request")

A request which can be executed with `Client::execute()`.

[RequestBuilder](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")

A builder to construct the properties of a `Request`.

[Response](struct.Response.html "struct rquest::Response")

A Response to a submitted `Request`.

[SslCurve](struct.SslCurve.html "struct rquest::SslCurve")

A TLS Curve.

[StatusCode](struct.StatusCode.html "struct rquest::StatusCode")

An HTTP status code (`status-code` in RFC 9110 et al.).

[StreamDependency](struct.StreamDependency.html "struct rquest::StreamDependency")

Represents a stream dependency in HTTP/2 priority frames.

[StreamId](struct.StreamId.html "struct rquest::StreamId")

A stream identifier, as described in [Section 5.1.1](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7540#section-5.1.1)
 of RFC 7540.

[Upgraded](struct.Upgraded.html "struct rquest::Upgraded")

An upgraded HTTP connection.

[Url](struct.Url.html "struct rquest::Url")

A parsed URL record.

[Utf8Bytes](struct.Utf8Bytes.html "struct rquest::Utf8Bytes")

UTF-8 wrapper for [Bytes](https://docs.rs/bytes/1.10.1/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bytes/bytes/struct.Bytes.html "struct bytes::bytes::Bytes")
.

[Version](struct.Version.html "struct rquest::Version")

Represents a version of the HTTP spec.

[WebSocket](struct.WebSocket.html "struct rquest::WebSocket")

A websocket connection

[WebSocketRequestBuilder](struct.WebSocketRequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::WebSocketRequestBuilder")

Wrapper for [`RequestBuilder`](struct.RequestBuilder.html "struct rquest::RequestBuilder")
 that performs the websocket handshake when sent.

[WebSocketResponse](struct.WebSocketResponse.html "struct rquest::WebSocketResponse")

The serverŌĆÖs response to the websocket upgrade request.

*    [![0x676e67](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/51810656?v=4)](https://crates.io/users/0x676e67)

rquest
======

[![CI](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
 [![Crates.io License](https://img.shields.io/crates/l/rquest)](./LICENSE)
 ![Crates.io MSRV](https://img.shields.io/crates/msrv/rquest) [![crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/rquest.svg)](https://crates.io/crates/rquest)
 [![Crates.io Total Downloads](https://img.shields.io/crates/d/rquest)](https://crates.io/crates/rquest)

> ­¤ÜĆ Help me work seamlessly with open source sharing by [sponsoring me on GitHub](https://github.com/0x676e67/0x676e67/blob/main/SPONSOR.md)

An ergonomic all-in-one HTTP client for browser emulation with TLS, JA3/JA4, and HTTP/2 fingerprints.

Features
--------

*   Plain bodies, JSON, urlencoded, multipart
*   Cookie Store
*   Header Order
*   Redirect Policy
*   Rotating Proxies
*   WebSocket Upgrade
*   HTTPS via [BoringSSL](#building)
    
*   [Emulation](#emulation)
     Fingerprint

Example
-------

This asynchronous example utilizes [Tokio](https://tokio.rs)
 with optional features enabled, requiring the following configuration in Cargo.toml:

    [dependencies]
    tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
    rquest = "4"
    rquest-util = "1"
    

And then the code:

    use rquest::Client;
    use rquest_util::Emulation;
    
    #[tokio::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rquest::Error> {
        // Build a client
        let client = Client::builder()
            .emulation(Emulation::Firefox136)
            .build()?;
    
        // Use the API you're already familiar with
        let resp = client.get("https://tls.peet.ws/api/all").send().await?;
        println!("{}", resp.text().await?);
    
        Ok(())
    }
    

Emulation
---------

*   **HTTP/2 over TLS**
    
    **JA3**/**JA4**/**Akamai** fingerprints cannot accurately simulate browser fingerprints due to the sophistication of TLS encryption and the popularity of HTTP/2. `rquest` does not plan to support parsing these fingerprint strings for simulation. Users are encouraged to customize the configuration according to their own needs.
    
*   **Emulation Device**
    
    In fact, most device models have the same `TLS`/`HTTP2` configuration, except that the `User-Agent` is changed.
    
    Device emulation is maintained by [rquest-util](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest-util)
    .
    
    | **Browser** | **Versions** |
    | --- | --- |
    | **Chrome** | `Chrome100`, `Chrome101`, `Chrome104`, `Chrome105`, `Chrome106`, `Chrome107`, `Chrome108`, `Chrome109`, `Chrome114`, `Chrome116`, `Chrome117`, `Chrome118`, `Chrome119`, `Chrome120`, `Chrome123`, `Chrome124`, `Chrome126`, `Chrome127`, `Chrome128`, `Chrome129`, `Chrome130`, `Chrome131`, `Chrome132`, `Chrome133`, `Chrome134` |
    | **Edge** | `Edge101`, `Edge122`, `Edge127`, `Edge131`, `Edge134` |
    | **Safari** | `SafariIos17_2`, `SafariIos17_4_1`, `SafariIos16_5`, `Safari15_3`, `Safari15_5`, `Safari15_6_1`, `Safari16`, `Safari16_5`, `Safari17_0`, `Safari17_2_1`, `Safari17_4_1`, `Safari17_5`, `Safari18`, `SafariIPad18`, `Safari18_2`, `Safari18_1_1`, `Safari18_3` |
    | **OkHttp** | `OkHttp3_9`, `OkHttp3_11`, `OkHttp3_13`, `OkHttp3_14`, `OkHttp4_9`, `OkHttp4_10`, `OkHttp4_12`, `OkHttp5` |
    | **Firefox** | `Firefox109`, `Firefox117`, `Firefox128`, `Firefox133`, `Firefox135`, `FirefoxPrivate135`, `FirefoxAndroid135`, `Firefox136`, `FirefoxPrivate136` |
    

Building
--------

Avoid compiling with packages that depend on `openssl-sys`, as it shares the same prefix symbol with `boring-sys`, potentially leading to [link failures](https://github.com/cloudflare/boring/issues/197)
 and other issues. Even if compilation succeeds, using both `openssl-sys` and `boring-sys` together can result in memory segmentation faults. Until the upstream Boring resolves these linking conflicts, using `rustls` is the best workaround.

Install the dependencies required to build [BoringSSL](https://github.com/google/boringssl/blob/master/BUILDING.md#build-prerequisites)

    sudo apt-get install build-essential cmake perl pkg-config libclang-dev musl-tools -y
    
    cargo build --release
    

This GitHub Actions [workflow](.github/compilation-guide/build.yml)
 can be used to compile the project on **Linux**, **Windows**, and **macOS**.

.

#### rquest's sandbox limits

All the builds on docs.rs are executed inside a sandbox with limited resources. The limits for this crate are the following:

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Available RAM | 6.44 GB |
| Maximum rustdoc execution time | 15 minutes |
| Maximum size of a build log | 102.40 kB |
| Network access | blocked |
| Maximum number of build targets | 10  |

If a build fails because it hit one of those limits please [open an issue](https://github.com/rust-lang/docs.rs/issues/new/choose)
 to get them increased.

[rquest](index.html)

Struct ProxyCopy item path
==========================

[Source](../src/rquest/proxy.rs.html#71-74)

    pub struct Proxy { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

Configuration of a proxy that a `Client` should pass requests to.

A `Proxy` has a couple pieces to it:

*   a URL of how to talk to the proxy
*   rules on what `Client` requests should be directed to the proxy

For instance, letŌĆÖs look at `Proxy::http`:

    let proxy = rquest::Proxy::http("https://secure.example")?;

This proxy will intercept all HTTP requests, and make use of the proxy at `https://secure.example`. A request to `http://hyper.rs` will talk to your proxy. A request to `https://hyper.rs` will not.

Multiple `Proxy` rules can be configured for a `Client`. The `Client` will check each `Proxy` in the order it was added. This could mean that a `Proxy` added first with eager intercept rules, such as `Proxy::all`, would prevent a `Proxy` later in the list from ever working, so take care.

By enabling the `"socks"` feature it is possible to use a socks proxy:

    let proxy = rquest::Proxy::http("socks5://192.168.1.1:9000")?;

Implementations[┬¦](#implementations)

-------------------------------------

[Source](../src/rquest/proxy.rs.html#301-605)
[┬¦](#impl-Proxy)

### impl [Proxy](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")

[Source](../src/rquest/proxy.rs.html#316-320)

#### pub fn [http](#method.http)
<U: IntoProxyScheme>(proxy\_scheme: U) -> [Result](type.Result.html "type rquest::Result")
<[Proxy](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")
\>

Proxy all HTTP traffic to the passed URL.

##### [┬¦](#example)
Example

    let client = rquest::Client::builder()
        .proxy(rquest::Proxy::http("https://my.prox")?)
        .build()?;

[Source](../src/rquest/proxy.rs.html#336-340)

#### pub fn [https](#method.https)
<U: IntoProxyScheme>(proxy\_scheme: U) -> [Result](type.Result.html "type rquest::Result")
<[Proxy](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")
\>

Proxy all HTTPS traffic to the passed URL.

##### [┬¦](#example-1)
Example

    let client = rquest::Client::builder()
        .proxy(rquest::Proxy::https("https://example.prox:4545")?)
        .build()?;

[Source](../src/rquest/proxy.rs.html#356-360)

#### pub fn [all](#method.all)
<U: IntoProxyScheme>(proxy\_scheme: U) -> [Result](type.Result.html "type rquest::Result")
<[Proxy](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")
\>

Proxy **all** traffic to the passed URL.

##### [┬¦](#example-2)
Example

    let client = rquest::Client::builder()
        .proxy(rquest::Proxy::all("http://pro.xy")?)
        .build()?;

[Source](../src/rquest/proxy.rs.html#383-391)

#### pub fn [custom](#method.custom)
<F, U: IntoProxyScheme>(fun: F) -> [Proxy](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")

where F: [Fn](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.Fn.html "trait core::ops::function::Fn")
(&[Url](struct.Url.html "struct rquest::Url")
) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<U> + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + [Sync](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sync.html "trait core::marker::Sync")
 + 'static,

Provide a custom function to determine what traffic to proxy to where.

##### [┬¦](#example-3)
Example

    let target = rquest::Url::parse("https://my.prox")?;
    let client = rquest::Client::builder()
        .proxy(rquest::Proxy::custom(move |url| {
            if url.host_str() == Some("hyper.rs") {
                Some(target.clone())
            } else {
                None
            }
        }))
        .build()?;

[Source](../src/rquest/proxy.rs.html#441-444)

#### pub fn [basic\_auth](#method.basic_auth)
(self, username: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
, password: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
) -> [Proxy](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")

Set the `Proxy-Authorization` header using Basic auth.

##### [┬¦](#example-4)
Example

    let proxy = rquest::Proxy::https("http://localhost:1234")?
        .basic_auth("Aladdin", "open sesame");

[Source](../src/rquest/proxy.rs.html#460-463)

#### pub fn [custom\_http\_auth](#method.custom_http_auth)
(self, header\_value: [HeaderValue](header/struct.HeaderValue.html "struct rquest::header::HeaderValue")
) -> [Proxy](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")

Set the `Proxy-Authorization` header to a specified value.

##### [┬¦](#example-5)
Example

    let proxy = rquest::Proxy::https("http://localhost:1234")?
        .custom_http_auth(HeaderValue::from_static("justletmeinalreadyplease"));

[Source](../src/rquest/proxy.rs.html#496-499)

#### pub fn [custom\_http\_headers](#method.custom_http_headers)
(self, headers: [HeaderMap](header/struct.HeaderMap.html "struct rquest::header::HeaderMap")
) -> [Proxy](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")

Set custom HTTP headers to be used with the proxy.

This method allows you to specify custom HTTP headers that will be included in requests sent through the proxy. This can be useful for adding additional headers required by the proxy server, such as custom authentication headers or other metadata.

##### [┬¦](#parameters)
Parameters

*   `headers`: A `HeaderMap` containing the custom headers to be set.

##### [┬¦](#example-6)
Example

    let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();
    headers.insert("X-Custom-Header", HeaderValue::from_static("custom_value"));
    
    let proxy = rquest::Proxy::https("http://localhost:1234")?
        .custom_http_headers(headers);

This example demonstrates how to set a custom header `X-Custom-Header` with the value `custom_value` for the proxy.

[Source](../src/rquest/proxy.rs.html#514-517)

#### pub fn [no\_proxy](#method.no_proxy)
(self, no\_proxy: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[NoProxy](struct.NoProxy.html "struct rquest::NoProxy")
\>) -> [Proxy](struct.Proxy.html "struct rquest::Proxy")

Adds a `No Proxy` exclusion list to this Proxy

##### [┬¦](#example-7)
Example

    let proxy = rquest::Proxy::https("http://localhost:1234")?
        .no_proxy(rquest::NoProxy::from_string("direct.tld, sub.direct2.tld"));

 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")

 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")

============

rquest
======

[![CI](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
 [![Crates.io License](https://img.shields.io/crates/l/rquest)](./LICENSE)
 ![Crates.io MSRV](https://img.shields.io/crates/msrv/rquest) [![crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/rquest.svg)](https://crates.io/crates/rquest)
 [![Crates.io Total Downloads](https://img.shields.io/crates/d/rquest)](https://crates.io/crates/rquest)

> ­¤ÜĆ Help me work seamlessly with open source sharing by [sponsoring me on GitHub](https://github.com/0x676e67/0x676e67/blob/main/SPONSOR.md)

An ergonomic all-in-one HTTP client for browser emulation with TLS, JA3/JA4, and HTTP/2 fingerprinting.

Features
--------

*   Plain, JSON, urlencoded, multipart bodies
*   Header Order
*   Redirect Policy
*   Cookie Store
*   Rotating Proxies
*   WebSocket Upgrade
*   HTTPS via BoringSSL
*   Perfectly Chrome, Safari, and Firefox

Example
-------

This asynchronous example utilizes [Tokio](https://tokio.rs)
 with optional features enabled, requiring the following configuration in Cargo.toml:

    [dependencies]
    tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
    rquest = "3.0.3"
    rquest-util = "0.2.2"
    

And then the code:

    use rquest::Client;
    use rquest_util::Emulation;
    
    #[tokio::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rquest::Error> {
        // Build a client
        let client = Client::builder()
            .emulation(Emulation::Firefox135)
            .build()?;
    
        // Use the API you're already familiar with
        let resp = client.get("https://tls.peet.ws/api/all").send().await?;
        println!("{}", resp.text().await?);
    
        Ok(())
    }
    

Emulation
---------

*   **HTTP/2 over TLS**
    
    **JA3**/**JA4**/**Akamai** fingerprints cannot accurately simulate browser fingerprints due to the sophistication of TLS encryption and the popularity of HTTP/2. `rquest` does not plan to support parsing these fingerprint strings for simulation. Users are encouraged to customize the configuration according to their own needs.
    
*   **Emulation Device**
    
    In fact, most device models have the same `TLS`/`HTTP2` configuration, except that the `User-Agent` is changed.
    
    Device emulation is maintained by [rquest-util](https://github.com/0x676e67/rquest-util)
    .
    
    | **Browser** | **Versions** |
    | --- | --- |
    | **Chrome** | `Chrome100`, `Chrome101`, `Chrome104`, `Chrome105`, `Chrome106`, `Chrome107`, `Chrome108`, `Chrome109`, `Chrome114`, `Chrome116`, `Chrome117`, `Chrome118`, `Chrome119`, `Chrome120`, `Chrome123`, `Chrome124`, `Chrome126`, `Chrome127`, `Chrome128`, `Chrome129`, `Chrome130`, `Chrome131`, `Chrome132`, `Chrome133` |
    | **Edge** | `Edge101`, `Edge122`, `Edge127`, `Edge131` |
    | **Safari** | `SafariIos17_2`, `SafariIos17_4_1`, `SafariIos16_5`, `Safari15_3`, `Safari15_5`, `Safari15_6_1`, `Safari16`, `Safari16_5`, `Safari17_0`, `Safari17_2_1`, `Safari17_4_1`, `Safari17_5`, `Safari18`, `SafariIPad18`, `Safari18_2`, `Safari18_1_1` |
    | **OkHttp** | `OkHttp3_9`, `OkHttp3_11`, `OkHttp3_13`, `OkHttp3_14`, `OkHttp4_9`, `OkHttp4_10`, `OkHttp5` |
    | **Firefox** | `Firefox109`, `Firefox117`, `Firefox128`, `Firefox133`, `Firefox135`, `FirefoxPrivate135`, `FirefoxAndroid135` |
    

Building
--------

Avoid compiling with packages that depend on `openssl-sys`, as it shares the same prefix symbol with `boring-sys`, potentially leading to [link failures](https://github.com/cloudflare/boring/issues/197)
 and other issues. Even if compilation succeeds, using both `openssl-sys` and `boring-sys` together can result in memory segmentation faults. Until the upstream Boring resolves these linking conflicts, using `rustls` is the best workaround.

Install the dependencies required to build [BoringSSL](https://github.com/google/boringssl/blob/master/BUILDING.md#build-prerequisites)

    sudo apt-get install build-essential cmake perl pkg-config libclang-dev musl-tools -y
    
    cargo build --release
    

This GitHub Actions [workflow](.github/compilation-guide/build.yml)
 can be used to compile the project on **Linux**, **Windows**, and **macOS**.

.

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[┬¦](#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[┬¦](#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1.15.0/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
